occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): Our group took a whale shark tour, traveling from the port at 0800 around 1 hour to Parque Nacional Isla Contoy ($10 fee to enter). We drove around for about 45 minutes before the guide said this individual was spotted by another boat. When we got on scene just after 0930 over 8 vessels were taking turns letting 2 people enter the water to swim with this shark. We waited patiently for about another hour until we got to see this amazing creature. By the time our group left at 1130 there were over 12 vessels waiting to see the only whale shark for the day. The guides were respectful to each other and enforced that only 2 people can swim at a time and not to touch the animal. We got within a meter from it at one point as it surfaced to forage on plankton. It appeared to be indifferent to the people and boats around it. The tour guide said that peak season is July, which ironically is also hurricane season when it gets more troublesome. (2025-05-28)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): My first record of a feeding surface in Timor-Leste water. Last week, a whale shark (Rhincodon typus) was photographed and filmed in a feeding frenzy with silky sharks and a swarm of seabirds, including terns and boobies. This spectacular event was likely triggered by significant upwelling, which brought nutrient-rich waters and baitfish to the surface, attracting predators from above and below. The interaction highlights the dynamic energy of the ocean and the intricate relationships within marine ecosystems during such rare feeding events. (2024-12-02)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): This is the young whale shark which washed up on Double Island Beach yesterday, tide has rolled it over during the night and it has additional injuries on it now from other fish/birds feeding on it overnight. From the markings, they are saying it is the same whale shark that was beaching itself on Jan 27th at Little Cove, off Noosa National Park. The carcass was still laying on the beach when we returned home late this afternoon. Lots of people at the beach taking photo's of it. (2021-02-13)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): Tanjung Lang, north of Pulau Redang. Depth of the dive site is around 25m, but whale shark was sighted at the surface between 1m - 5m. At one time, it dive down to almost 20 meters and away from us, and was out of sight, but in less than a minute, it came back, circling us. Specimen was very curious and circled a group of 15+ divers and 2 dive boats, for more than 30 minutes, sucking in and playing with bubbles from scuba divers. Specimen is accompanied by Remoras (Echeneis Naucrates) and Cobias (Rachycentron Canadum) (2020-07-10)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): Off Redang, Pulau Lima - Southern Tip. One whale shark was sighted between 1m - 10m. Circling a group of 6 divers. sucking in and playing with bubbles from scuba divers. After about 20 minutes, another whale shark was sighted. Both whale sharks circled us for more than an hour. We had to leave eventually, while the whale sharks are still circling our boat. There has been several sightings of whale shark around Pulau Redang, though it's not common. And to have 2 whale shark apearing at the same time is rare. Specimen is accompanied by Remoras (Echeneis Naucrates) and Cobias (Rachycentron Canadum) (2020-10-19)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): At Donsol (refused to go to Oslob). Maybe 7 meters. (2016-05-02)
Seeing a whale shark for the first time was perhaps like seeing a dinosaur. (2016-05-02)

Swimming side by side with these creatures was a humble experience. I'm well into the foreground here. The whale shark was estimated at about 6 meters. We saw 12 in two days at Donsol ranging in size from 4.5~11 meters long. (2016-05-03)

It's a shame that the only photo I have of this particular whake shark off Donsol is its dorsal fin (with myself off to the side). You can see the back was gnarled by a propellar. She was enormous. The Captain said 12m but I believe 11m is more accurate. Regardless, at 183 cm, I felt like a tiny shrimp next to her. She allowed us to stay with her for a good 15 or 20 minutes before disappearing into the deep. (2016-05-03)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): Tiburón Ballena, animal de aprox. 6 a 8 metros de longitud, de caracter sumamente apacible, de nado superficial lento y tranquilo, que mantiene su boca abierta para alimentarse del plancton y pequeños peces de la superficie. No parece inmutarse de la presencia humana, aún a pesar de los involuntarios toques al nadar en su cercanía. ...Seguramente se le perturba por tantas lanchas y turistas que los quiere ver y contemplar nadando. (2015-08-01)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate):
Whale Shark - Misty Cliffs


Washed up on beach overnight - about 5 or 6m long. Still alive when washed up, but dead within hours. No obvious signs of trauma, but facial area very bloody and possibly had a skirmish with pilchard trawlers which were active in the area overnight. Inshore water temperature about 11 degs C, possibly warmer a bit further out.

(2014-03-17)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): I've had this whale shark entered into the whaleshark.org database and nicknamed him Bruno. He approached our boat during our dive day's surface interval around noon. By my estimate he measured around 5m long that time. This individual seemed very curious of us. While I was in the water with him, he looked like he was "sniffing" the underside of the boat, as if waiting for something. My guess is this guy's been to Oslob, Cebu, where they feed whale sharks for the enjoyment of tourists. (2012-02-27)

occurrenceRemarks (eventDate): The biggest of all fish - the almighty whale shark. These animals congregate every year around April to June in Ningaloo Reef in Western Australia. Truly one of the most memorable creatures which you can meet in the deep blue. As I have only seen a few as of now, everyone got its own name. This one is Bob and I get a message that he has been spotted now and then again... (2012-04-24)
No puede estar seguro de la ubicación porque fue observado en un tour (2012-05-24)
